primary_contact.name: Gene Goodman
primary_contact.phone: (856) 895-9667 (main line, secondary direct number obtained during call)
primary_contact.context_notes: Gene came up multiple times in Google reviews as 'Operations Chief' and is described by customers as kind, efficient, and available. He is highly trusted by customers and has decision authority on operational matters. He referred to bringing the website project forward himself; deciding party but likely confirms with Arthur Carroll (owner) before final sign-off.

extended_service_area: South Jersey (Cherry Hill, Mt. Laurel, Marlton, Burlington, Willingboro, Beverly, Riverside, Mt. Holly, Medford Lakes, Berlin, Sicklerville, Blackwood, Williamstown, Glassboro, Swedesboro, Mullica Hill, Salem, Millville, Vineland, Elmer, Cape May, Voorhees, Camden, Wenonah, Bellmawr, Carneys Point, Marlboro)

leadership.name: Arthur Carroll
leadership.role: Owner / Primary phone answerer / Field consultant

leadership.name: Gene Goodman
leadership.role: Operations Chief / Primary contact for Elenos engagement

team_members_named_in_reviews.note: Team is named explicitly and frequently in customer reviews. This is a HUGE signal — customers connect with named individuals, not anonymous techs. Site should feature team prominently with photos and bios where possible.

primary_services: Electrical panel replacement and upgrades (150A to 200A is most common)
Outlet, switch, GFCI installation and repair
Storm damage repair and insurance claim coordination
services.free_consultations: Yes — free phone consultations with master electricians, often resolves issues without a service call

core_value_propositions: Live master electrician answers calls 24/7/365 — no answering service, no robots
Same-day or next-day service for non-emergencies
Often resolves issues over the phone at no cost
Master electricians only — not journeymen techs
Professional, courteous, leaves job sites clean
Consultative approach — will tell you NOT to hire them if a fix is simple enough to DIY
differentiators.brand_voice_in_reviews: The owner's review responses use phrases like 'help people protect their lives and property with the safe use of electricity' as a clear mission statement. Tone is grateful, warm, family-business-coded, and emphasizes customer relationship over transaction. Use of phrases like 'going the extra mile,' 'the safe use of electricity,' and '24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year' is repeated and on-message.

primary_pains_solved: Power loss in middle of the night with no electrician answering elsewhere
Burning smell, sparking outlets, fire safety concerns
Outdated electrical panels (fuse boxes, Federal Pacific, glass fuses, dated breakers)
Insurance company runaround during storm damage
Other electricians charging weekend dispatch fees or refusing to come out
Confusion about whether issue is utility company's responsibility or homeowner's
Vulnerable customers (elderly parents, medical equipment dependents, families with kids) needing fast restoration
customer_pain_points_observed.common_customer_emotional_state: Scared, panicked, or frustrated when calling. Often late at night, often after multiple other electricians refused or didn't answer. Lyons' competitive edge is calm, professional reassurance + fast action.

competitive_context.previous_web_vendor: Comfort Media Group — relationship is over. Gene mentioned moving in a different direction. Worth understanding why during the project.
competitive_context.pricing_position: Premium-priced. Two negative reviews specifically called out pricing as 'more than double' competitors. Owner responses defend pricing on quality, availability, and value grounds. This is a feature, not a bug — Lyons does not compete on price.
competitive_context.key_competitors_implied: Other South NJ electricians (most competitors don't answer 24/7, don't offer financing, don't have 5-star track record at scale). Specific competitors not named in reviews.

vision_for_new_website.expressed_by_gene: Wants something brand new on the web. Not just informational — wants RESOURCES section AND an AI chatbot that can answer questions and connect people to an electrician.
interpreted_needs: Site needs to reflect 5-star quality — premium look, not generic contractor template
Strong emergency-services emphasis — 'CALL NOW' should be impossible to miss on mobile
Resources / education section to position Lyons as the trusted expert (helps with SEO and trust)
Real team featured — photos and names of named electricians
Reviews integration — 5.0 / 436 reviews must be prominently displayed
Service area map — visually convey the regional reach
AI chatbot — vision is broad, scope must be defined carefully

ai_chatbot_scope_consideration.gene_articulated_vision: Chatbot to answer questions and connect people to an electrician.
ai_chatbot_scope_consideration.elenos_recommended_approach: Phase the chatbot. Phase 1 of project = website with chatbot-ready foundation. Phase 2 of engagement (within retainer or separate scope) = build the chatbot once we have actual customer questions captured to train it on. This produces a better chatbot AND reduces first-build risk for Elenos.
potential_chatbot_capabilities: Triage emergency vs. non-emergency calls (route emergencies to phone, schedule non-emergencies)
Capture name, address, problem description, urgency level
Provide common DIY troubleshooting (when to call utility vs. electrician)
Surface relevant resources from the resources section
Hand off to a live human via phone, SMS, or live chat for emergency cases
Should NEVER give specific electrical advice that could create safety/liability issues
ai_chatbot_scope_consideration.liability_consideration: Electrical advice from a chatbot creates real liability. Chatbot must have hardcoded safety responses for dangerous situations (sparks, fire, water + electrical, exposed wires) that route to immediate phone contact, not chatbot-attempted advice.

deal_structure.premium_pricing_justified_by: Custom AI chatbot scope, larger site (multi-page with resources), team features, regional service area, 5-star reputation deserves premium positioning, ongoing retainer work
retainer_inclusions_to_define: Monthly content updates / new pages as services expand
Up to X hours per month of additional development
deal_structure.timeline_proposed: 4-6 weeks from signed proposal to launch
next_milestones: Demo + proposal delivered next week (call scheduled)

primary_pages: Homepage — emergency-first hero, 'Call now 24/7,' 5.0 stars badge, service area, why Lyons
Emergency Services — dedicated page with biggest 'CALL NOW' button, common emergency types, what to do before they arrive
Services — full service list with detail pages for major categories (panel upgrades, service line, rewiring, etc.)
About / Our Team — family business story, 29+ years, named electricians with photos
Service Area — interactive map of South NJ, PA, DE coverage
Resources / Education — articles, FAQs, when to call vs. DIY, common issues explained (chatbot training source)
Reviews — all 436+ reviews integrated, sorted, searchable
Contact / Schedule — booking flow for non-emergency, prominent phone for emergency
key_features: Mobile-first design — emergency callers are on phones, this is non-negotiable
Persistent 'Call Now 24/7' button on every page (especially on mobile)
Click-to-call functionality everywhere phone numbers appear
Reviews carousel pulling live from Google
SSL, fast load times (under 3 seconds), accessibility compliance
SEO-optimized for 'emergency electrician [city] NJ' across service area

design_direction.visual_language: Premium, trustworthy, professional. Not cheap-contractor-template. Not corporate-stiff. Family business meets craftsman quality.
design_direction.color_palette_to_consider: Their current site likely has electric blue and yellow / safety colors. New design should keep electrical-industry color codes (yellow = caution, blue = trust, red = emergency) but elevate them. Possibly a deep navy + warm accent + clean whites. Avoid generic blue-and-orange contractor palette.
design_direction.imagery: Real photos of the team, real photos of their trucks, real photos of completed work. NOT stock photos. Their reputation is built on REAL people.
design_direction.typography: Serious, readable, trustworthy. Sans-serif primary, possibly with a serif accent for headlines. Avoid trendy display fonts.
design_direction.tone_in_copy: Mirror Arthur's review-response voice. Mission-driven ('help people protect their lives and property with the safe use of electricity'). Warm, grateful, professional. Family business coded.

trust_signals_to_feature: 5.0 stars / 436 Google reviews — feature prominently above the fold

elenos_engagement_notes.why_this_deal_is_significant: Lyons is Elenos's first qualified deal. Premium-priced ($4K + $750/mo = $13K year-1 value). Premium client (5.0 / 436 reviews, 29+ years, regional reach). Sets the reputation foundation for future Elenos prospects. Successful delivery here is the case study that closes Elenos clients 2-5.
elenos_engagement_notes.scope_risk_to_manage: AI chatbot agreed in scope but Elenos has not yet built one in production. Recommend Phase 1 (website with chatbot-ready foundation) + Phase 2 (chatbot deployment after launch, trained on real conversations). This is honest, technically correct, and protects delivery quality.
elenos_engagement_notes.ai_receptionist_future_opportunity: Lyons is a perfect fit for Elenos's eventual AI receptionist offer (Phase 2 of summer roadmap). Emergency-services business with 24/7 phone reach as core differentiator. Future upsell potential: $5K setup + $300-500/mo retainer for AI receptionist that handles overflow + after-hours triage. Do NOT mention this until website is delivered and trust is earned. Likely opportunity for Q3 2026 conversation.
elenos_engagement_notes.demo_deliverable_for_next_week: Figma or Framer designed mockup of homepage + 1-2 inner pages. Custom to Lyons (their colors, their team photos if available, their service area). Walk Gene through live on screen-share. Goal: visual confirmation of direction, leading into proposal sign-off.
elenos_engagement_notes.proposal_to_send_with_demo: 1-2 page proposal: recap of Gene's vision in his words, what Elenos will build, scope (Phase 1 website + Phase 2 chatbot), timeline (4-6 weeks for Phase 1), investment ($4,000 setup + $750/mo retainer with retainer inclusions clearly listed), payment terms (50% / 50%), next steps (sign + Stripe deposit).

open_questions_for_next_call: Confirm Gene's role — is he the final decision-maker or does Arthur sign off?
Confirm if Arthur should be on the next call
What didn't work with Comfort Media Group? What needs to be different?
Walk through the proposed Phase 1 / Phase 2 chatbot sequencing — is that OK with Gene's vision?
Get specific on emergency triage flow — what's the current path when someone calls at 11 PM?
Confirm service area for SEO targeting — focus on which 5-10 cities first?
Confirm: do they want online booking for non-emergency work, or keep all leads going through phone?
Confirm: any branding assets (logo files, fonts, brand guide) from previous vendor?
Confirm: any photos of team / trucks / completed work available for site, or do we need a photoshoot?

metadata.context_doc_purpose: Single source of truth for the Lyons Electrical engagement. Used by Elenos to build the demo, proposal, and eventual site. Update as new information emerges during follow-up calls and project work.
metadata.next_update_trigger: After demo + proposal call next week
